rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs' Motion To Have Plaintiffs' Requests Deemed Admitted, To Strike General Electric Company'S Objections, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to have plaintiffs¿ requests for admission deemed admitted and to strike defendant General Electric¿s objections to plaintiffs¿ written discovery is denied. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate they sufficiently tried to resolve this issue without court intervention as required by the parties¿ stipulated order. Further, the requests for admission will not be deemed admitted because defendant served its verifications prior to the hearing on this motion. (C.C.P. 2033.280.) Defendant did not waive its objections because it served timely objections and no verifications are required for objections. (C.C.P. 2030.250, 2031.250, 2033.240; Food 4 Less Supermarkets, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 651.) Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
